S3000L-B6568-03000-00Applicable to: All S3000L-A-19-00-0000-00A-040A-AChap 19DMC-S3000L-A-19-00-0000-00A-040A-A_000_02_EN-US.doc 2009-06-08 Page 51The Maintenance_man_hours_per_operating_hour value is always defined for a specificLSA_candidate (inherited from class Key_performance_parameter)An association with one or many Operators (ie customers or users) that approved theMaintenance_man_hours_per_operating_hour value (inherited from classKey_performance_parameter).Maintenance_man_hours_per_operating_hour special recommendations:An LSA_candidate can have multiple defined Maintenance_man_hours_per_operating_hours depending on operational environment, etc. If multipleMaintenance_man_hours_per_ operating_hours are defined for the same type of keyperformance parameter (eg contractApplicability_statements as defined in UoF Applicability Statement.Representations of the value for the Maintenance_man_hours_per_operating_hourattribute are recommended to use the Value_with_limit representation, whereValue_with_tolerances or Value_range_property is valid.4.9.3.8 Mean_time_between_unscheduled_removalThe Mean_time_between_unscheduled_removal class is a specialization ofKey_performance_parameter, and represents the total number of operational units (eg, miles,rounds, hours) divided by the total number of items removed from that system during a statedperiod of time. This term is defined to exclude removals as either being scheduled or performedin order to facilitate other maintenance and removals for product improvement.Mean_time_between_unscheduled_removal attributes:Key_performance_parameter_type (inherited from class Key_performance_parameter)Key_performance_parameter_method (inherited from class Key_performance_parameter)Mean_time_between_unscheduled_removal (the value itself)Mean_time_between_unscheduled_removal associations:The Mean_time_between_unscheduled_removal value is always defined for a specificLSA_candidate (inherited from class Key_performance_parameter).An association with one or many Operators (ie customers or users) that approved theMean_time_between_unscheduled_removal value (inherited from classKey_performance_parameter).Mean_time_between_unscheduled_removal special recommendations:An LSA_candidate can have multiple defined Mean_time_between_unscheduled_removalsdepending on operational environment, etc. If multiple Mean_time_between_unscheduled_removals are defined for the same type of key performance parameter (eg contractthese shall be distinguished by the assignment of Applicability_statements as defined inUoF Applicability Statement.Representations of the value for the Mean_time_between_unscheduled_removal attributeare recommended to use the Value_with_limit representation, where Limit_qualifier is set tose of Value_with_tolerances orValue_range_property is valid.4.9.3.9 Mean_time_to_repairThe Mean_time_to_repair class is a specialization of Key_performance_parameter, andrepresents the total elapsed time for corrective maintenance divided by the total number ofcorrective maintenance actions during a given period of time.Mean_time_to_repair attributes:Key_performance_parameter_type (inherited from class Key_performance_parameter)

S3000L-B6568-03000-00Applicable to: All S3000L-A-19-00-0000-00A-040A-AChap 19DMC-S3000L-A-19-00-0000-00A-040A-A_000_02_EN-US.doc 2009-06-08 Page 53Shop_processing_time (the value itself)Shop_processing_time associations:The Shop_processing_time value is defined for a specific LSA_candidate (inherited fromclass Key_performance_parameter)An association with one or many operators (ie customers or users) that approved theShop_processing_time value (inherited from class Key_performance_parameter).Shop_processing_time special recommendations:An LSA_candidate can have multiple defined Shop_processing_times depending onoperational environment, etc. If multiple Shop_processing_times are defined for the sametype of key performance parameter (eg "contracted"), these shall be distinguished by theassignment of Applicability_statements as defined in UoF Applicability statement.Representations of the value for the Shop_processing_time attribute are recommended touse the Value_with_limit representation, where Limit_qualifier is set to "maximum". Theremight also be cases where the use of Value_with_tolerances or Value_range_property isvalid.4.9.3.12 Mean_time_between_failureThe Mean_time_between_failure (MTBF) class is a specialization of Key_performance_parameter, where the MTBF is the total operational life of a population of LSA Candidatesdivided by the total number of failures within the population during a particular measurementinterval. The definition holds for time, rounds, miles, events, or other measure of life units.Mean_time_between_failure attributes:Key_performance_parameter_type (inherited from class Key_performance_parameter)Key_performance_parameter_method (inherited from class Key_performance_parameter)Mean_time_between_failure (the value itself)Mean_time_between_failure associations:The Mean_time_between_failure value is always defined for a specific LSA_candidate(inherited from class Key_performance_parameter)An association with one or many operators (ie customers or users) that approved theMean_time_between_failure value (inherited from class Key_performance_parameter)An optional association with zero, one or many instances of Mean_time_between_failure_correction.Mean_time_between_failure special recommendations:An LSA_candidate can have multiple defined Mean_time_between_failure depending onoperational environment, etc. If multiple Mean_time_between_failure are defined for thesame type of key performance parameter (eg "contracted"), these shall be distinguished bythe assignment of Applicability_statements as defined in UoF Applicability Statement.Representations of the value for the Mean_time_between_failure attribute arerecommended to use the Value_with_limit representation, where Limit_qualifier is set toValue_range_property is valid.4.9.3.13 Mean_time_between_failure_correctionThe Mean_time_beween_failure_correction class defines correction factors for an associatedmean time between failure value. Mean_time_between_failure_correction can be defined fromthe following perspectives:Usage of the associated LSA_candidate under specific conditions, eg environment whichcauses additional stress to the system (sand, extreme temperatures, salty environment)
```

**📝 核心内容**
```text
S3000L-B6568-03000-00Applicable to: All S3000L-A-19-00-0000-00A-040A-AChap 19DMC-S3000L-A-19-00-0000-00A-040A-A_000_02_EN-US.doc 2009-06-08 Page 55An optional association with zero, one or many instances of Mean_time_between_failure_correction4.10 UoF LSA Candidate Analysis Activity 4.10.1 Overall descriptionThe LSA Candidate Analysis Activity UoF supports the selection and justification of LogisticsSupport Analysis (LSA) tasks to be performed on the respective LSA Candidate.NoteLSA Candidate analysis activities shall only to be documented for those parts andBreakdown_element_revisions that are identified as being LSA Candidates.There is no requirement that all types of LSA tasks shall be performed for every single LSACandidate, but there is a requirement to document the decision made for each combinationof LSA Candidate and LSA task.

S3000L-B6568-03000-00Applicable to: All S3000L-A-19-00-0000-00A-040A-AChap 19DMC-S3000L-A-19-00-0000-00A-040A-A_000_02_EN-US.doc 2009-06-08 Page 564.10.2 Graphical representationICN-B6865-S3000L0224-001-01Fig 25 LSA Candidate Analysis Activity - class model4.10.3 UoF LSA Candidate Analysis Activity - New class and interface definitions4.10.3.1 Candidate_item_analysis_taskThe Candidate_item_analysis_task class identifies which LSA activities are to be performed forthe respective LSA Candidate, the rationale behind it, and the recorded status for the respectiveanalysis activity.
```

- 字数: 2418 (约1450 tokens)
- 分块类型: paragraph

**📝 核心内容**
```text
S3000L-B6568-03000-00Applicable to: All S3000L-A-19-00-0000-00A-040A-AChap 19DMC-S3000L-A-19-00-0000-00A-040A-A_000_02_EN-US.doc 2009-06-08 Page 57Each identified LSA activity can also be associated with zero, one or many documents, egdocuments containing the LSA activities result.NoteLSA activities are often selected during the LSA guidance conference.LSA activities can be defined for LSA Candidates at any level of indenture.The Candidate_item_analysis_task class is an abstract class, ie the class itself will neverbe instantiated but any of its subclasses can be. Subclasses of theCandidate_item_analysis_ task are:LSA_candidate_comparative_analysis_taskLSA_candidate_human_factor_analysis_taskLSA_candidate_realiability_analysis_taskLSA_candidate_maintainability_analysis_taskLSA_candidate_testability_analysis_taskLSA_candidate_failure_mode_and_effect_analysis_taskLSA_candidate_damage_analysis_taskLSA_candidate_special_event_analysis_taskLSA_candidate_damage_analysis_taskLSA_candidate_special_event_analysis_taskLSA_candidate_scheduled_maintenance_analysis_taskLSA_candidate_level_of_repair_analysis_taskLSA_candidate_mainenance_task_analysis_taskLSA_candidate_software_data_loading_analysis_taskLSA_candidate_software_support_analysis_taskLSA_candidate_operational_analysis_taskLSA_candidate_simulation_operational_scenarios_analysis_taskLSA_candidate_training_needs_analysis_taskLSA_candidate_other_analysis_taskCandidate_item_analysis_task attributes:Candidate_item_analysis_task_indicatorCandidate_item_analysis_task_rationaleCandidate_item_analysis_task_statusCandidate_item_analysis_task_dateAny subclass of Candidate_item_analysis_task class must also implement the followinginterface:Document_assignment_interface4.10.3.2 LSA_candidate_comparative_analysis_taskThe LSA_candidate_comparative_analysis_task class is a specialization ofCandidate_item_analysis_task, and records the decision made regarding comparative analysisfor the LSA Candidate under consideration.NoteThe use of class LSA_candidate_comparative_analysis_task has no meaning by itself, butonly in the context of a specific LSA_candidate.LSA_candidate_comparative_analysis attributes:Candidate_item_analysis_task_indicator (inherited from Candidate_item_analysis_task)Candidate_item_analysis_task_rationale (inherited from Candidate_item_analysis_task)Candidate_item_analysis_task_status (inherited from Candidate_item_analysis_task)
```

### 399

**📊 元数据**
- 所属条号: `段落399`
- 章节路径: `-`
- 字数: 1975 (约1185 tokens)
- 分块类型: paragraph

**📝 核心内容**
```text
S3000L-B6568-03000-00Applicable to: All S3000L-A-19-00-0000-00A-040A-AChap 19DMC-S3000L-A-19-00-0000-00A-040A-A_000_02_EN-US.doc 2009-06-08 Page 64An association with LSA_candidate_special_event_analysis_taskAn association with LSA_candidate_scheduled_maintenance_analysis_taskAn association with LSA_candidate_level_of_repair_analysis_taskAn association with LSA_candidate_mainenance_task_analysis_taskAn association with LSA_candidate_software_data_loading_analysis_taskAn association with LSA_candidate_software_support_analysis_taskAn association with LSA_candidate_operational_analysis_taskAn association with LSA_candidate_simulation_operational_scenarios_analysis_taskAn association with LSA_candidate_training_needs_analysis_taskAn association with LSA_candidate_other_analysis_taskNoteOnly those instances of Breakdown_element_revision and Part (ie those classes thatimplement the LSA_candidate interface) that are selected as LSA Candidates must identifywhich analysis activities are to be performed.4.11 UoF LSA-FMEA and Special Events 4.11.1 Overall descriptionThe LSA-FMEA and Special Events UoF supports the recording of portions of the results fromthe following LSA activities:LSA-FMEA (Failure Mode and Effect Analysis),Damage analysisSpecial event analysisTestability analysisThe basis for this UoF is that each LSA Candidate can perform a set of functions, where itsability to perform the function might be affected by a set of failure modes. A failure mode can becaused by an inherent failure or by a special event (induced failure).NoteThe LSA-FMEA focuses on non-critical failure modes, which may require some kind ofcorrective task. Critical failure modes are analyzed as part of the scheduled maintenanceanalysis activity and are out of scope for this UoF. However, the LSA-FMEA may build onthe results from scheduled maintenance analysis, eg MSG-3, RCM.Each failure mode can be associated with special means for failure mode detection andlocalization.
```

Experience shows thattechnologies used can be more or less sensitive to damage.NoteAn LSA Candidate can have zero, one or many technology behavior ratings over time.The use of class LSA_candidate_technology_behavior_rating has no meaning by itself, butonly in the context of an LSA_candidate.LSA_candidate_technology_behavior_rating attributes:Technology_behaviour_knowledge_ratingTechnology_sensitivity_rating

However, a software solutionmust also support the situation where function descriptions are uniquely defined for eachLSA Candidate under consideration, ie there is no reuse of function descriptions betweenLSA Candidates.Function attributes:Function_identifier (zero or one)Function_descriptionFunction_type (zero or one)Function association:An association with zero, one or many LSA_candidates that performs the function (via theLSA_candidate_function class)4.11.3.3 LSA_candidate_functionThe LSA_candidate_function class defines a relationship between an LSA_candidate and itsFunctions. It also associates possible LSA_candidate failure modes that can affect theLSA_candidate ability to perform the specified function.LSA_candidate_function associations:An association with the LSA_candidate that performs the specified functionAn association with the specified FunctionAn optional association with zero, one or many failure modes for the LSA_candidate (classLSA_candidate_failure_mode) that can affect its ability to perform the specified functionNoteThere is one instance of LSA_candidate_function per relevant combination ofLSA_candidate and Function.If there is a need to document failure modes for an LSA Candidate without defining aspecific function, one can create a dummy function eg "Function".4.11.3.4 LSA_candidate_failure_modeThe LSA_candidate_failure_mode class supports the definition of possible failure modes for aspecific LSA_candidate.NoteFailure mode is also known as damage for failure modes caused by special events.LSA_candidate_failure_mode attributes:Failure_mode_identifierFailure_mode_descriptionFailure_mode_occurrence_rating (one or many)LSA_candidate_failure_mode associations:

S3000L-B6568-03000-00Applicable to: All S3000L-A-19-00-0000-00A-040A-AChap 19DMC-S3000L-A-19-00-0000-00A-040A-A_000_02_EN-US.doc 2009-06-08 Page 704.11.3.12 TestabilityThe testability class defines a relationship between a Test_function and one or moreLSA_candidate_failure_modes which can be detected and/or localized, using the test function.NoteThere is one instance of testability per relevant combination of Test_function andLSA_candidate_failure_mode (via Failure_mode_detection_and_localization for thecorresponding LSA_candidate_failure_mode).Testability attributes:Test_detection_rateTest_isolation_rateTestability associations:An association with the specific Test_function that can be used for detecting and/orlocalizing a specific LSA_candidate_failure_mode (via Failure_mode_detection_and_localization).An association with the specified LSA_candidate_failure_mode that can be detected and/orlocalized by the Test_function (via Failure_mode_detection_and_localization).NoteTestability is defined per combination of Test_function and LSA_candidate_failure_mode(via Failure_mode_detection_and_localization).4.11.3.13 Test_function_implementationThe Test_function_implementation interface is implemented by the classes that can realize thetest function as such.Classes that implement the Test_function_implementation interface:Breakdown_elementPartClasses that implement the Test_function_implementation interface must implement thefollowing association:An optional association with zero, one or many instances of Test_function.4.11.4 UoF LSA-FMEA and Special Events - Additions to referenced class and interfacedefinitions4.11.4.1 PartThe part class defined in UoF Part, must also implement the following additional interface:Test_function_implementation4.11.4.2 Breakdown_elementThe Breakdown_element class defined in UoF Breakdown Structure must also implement thefollowing additional interface:Test_function_implementation4.11.4.3 LSA_candidateThe LSA_candidate interface defined in UoF LSA Candidate, must also implement the followingadditional association:An optional association with zero, one or many LSA_candidate_functionsAn optional association with zero, one or many LSA_candidate_technology_behavior_ratings

S3000L-B6568-03000-00Applicable to: All S3000L-A-19-00-0000-00A-040A-AChap 19DMC-S3000L-A-19-00-0000-00A-040A-A_000_02_EN-US.doc 2009-06-08 Page 714.12 UoF LSA Candidate Task Requirement 4.12.1 Overall descriptionThe LSA Candidate Task Requirement UoF supports early recordings of task requirements,which will be refined during the detailed maintenance task analysis, Software and data loadinganalysis etc.Objectives for documenting task requirements are, eg to:Produce input to the LSA review, and together with the customer define maintenanceconcepts for the respective LSA Candidate, before any detailed task analysis is beingcarried out.Identify product design changes that will result in increased maintainability, testability, etc.A task requirement can also be associated with the source for the requirement, ie the driver forthe task. The requirement for a task may be derived from any type of LSA activity.4.12.2 Graphical representationICN-B6865-S3000L0226-001-01Fig 27 LSA Candidate Task Requirement - class model4.12.3 UoF LSA Candidate Task Requirement - New class and interface definitions4.12.3.1 Task_requirementThe Task_requirement class supports the specification of task requirements prior to anydetailed task analysis.Task_requirement attributes:Task_requirement_identifierTask_requirement_revision_identifierTask_requirement_specification

S3000L-B6568-03000-00Applicable to: All S3000L-A-19-00-0000-00A-040A-AChap 19DMC-S3000L-A-19-00-0000-00A-040A-A_000_02_EN-US.doc 2009-06-08 Page 72Task_requirement_dateTask_requirement_decisionTask_requirement_special_resource (zero or one)Task_requirement associations:An association with one or many LSA_candidates for which the task is required (via Task_requirement_target)An optional association with zero, one or many succeeding revisions of theTask_requirementAn optional association with zero, one or many preceeding revison of the Task_requirementAn optional association with zero, one or many Candidate_item_analysis_tasks, whichforms the justification for the Task_requirementAn optional association with zero, one or many product design Change_requests, which arerequired in order to be able to carry out the required task in the specified way4.12.3.2 Task_requirement_changeThe Task_requirement_change class is an association class, ie it adds information to theassociation between two successive revisions of Task_requirement.Task_requirement_change attribute:Task_requirement_change_description (zero or one)4.12.3.3 Authority_driven_task_requirementThe Authority_driven_task_requirement class is a specialization of class Task_requirement, andshall be used to record task requirements which are derived from regulations and/or otherauthoritative sources.Authority_driven_task_requirement attributes:Task_requirement_specification (inherited from Task_requirement)Task_requirement_date (inherited from Task_requirement)Task_requirement_decision (inherited from Task_requirement)Task_requirement_special_resource (inherited from Task_requirement)Task_requirement_authorizing_organizationTask_requirement_authority_source_type (zero or one)Authority_driven_task_requirement associations:An association with one or many LSA_candidates for which the task is required (inheritedfrom Task_requirement)An optional association with zero, one or many Candidate_item_analysis_tasks, that is thesource for the task requirement (inherited from Task_requirement)An optional association with zero, one or many product design Change_requests, which arerequired in order to be able to carry out the required task in the specified way (inheritedfrom Task_requirement).Authority_driven_task_requirement special recommendations:Each instance of Authority_driven_task_requirement should have at least one documentreference from which the task requirement is derived, using the Document_assignmentability defined in UoF document (refer to Para 4.19) (using theDocument_assignment_interface).4.12.3.4 Task_requirement_targetThe Task_requirement_target class defines a relationship between a specific Task_requirementand the LSA_candidate for which the task is required.

### 408

**📊 元数据**
- 所属条号: `段落408`
- 章节路径: `-`
- 字数: 2400 (约1440 tokens)
- 分块类型: paragraph

**📝 核心内容**
```text
S3000L-B6568-03000-00Applicable to: All S3000L-A-19-00-0000-00A-040A-AChap 19DMC-S3000L-A-19-00-0000-00A-040A-A_000_02_EN-US.doc 2009-06-08 Page 73NoteThere is one instance of Task_requirement_target per relevant combination ofTask_requirement and LSA_candidate.Task_requirement_target associations:An association with a specific Task_requirement which is required for the LSA CandidateAn association with the LSA_candidate for which the task is requiredAn optional association with zero, one or many Task_limit requirements, defining, eg a tasklimit, as required by an authorityNoteThe Task_limit class is described in UoF Task Usage Part 1. Refer to Para 4.15.4.12.3.5 Change_requestThe Change_request class supports the recording of desired product design changes.Change_request attributes:Change_request_descriptionChange_request associations:An association with one or many specific Task_requirements from which the design changerequest has been derivedAn association with the LSA_candidate for which the design change request is defined4.12.4 UoF LSA Candidate Task Requirement - Additions to referenced class and interfacedefinitions4.12.4.1 LSA_candidateThe LSA_candidate interface defined in UoF LSA Candidate, must also implement the followingadditional associations:An optional association with zero, one or many Task_requirements (viaTask_requirement_target)An optional association with zero, one or many Change_requests4.12.4.2 Candidate_item_analysis_taskThe Candidate_item_analysis_task class defined in UoF LSA Candidate Analysis Activity, mustalso implement the following additional association:An optional association with zero, one or many Task_requirements, which refer to theCandidate_item_analysis_task as its justification4.13 UoF Task 4.13.1 Overall descriptionThe Task UoF supports a detailed definition of a task, both maintenance task and operationaltask.The Task procedure is described using a set of Subtasks. A Subtask can either be defined anddescribed within the Task under consideration, or make reference to another Task.NoteA Subtask that is defined and described within a Task cannot be referenced by any otherTask.There is also the possibility to define a time line (schedule) for the Subtasks contained within aTask. This can be used for the purpose of optimizing resource requirements and/or be the basisfor creating job cards in, eg, a fleet management system.
```

### 410

**📊 元数据**
- 所属条号: `段落410`
- 章节路径: `-`
- 字数: 2536 (约1521 tokens)
- 分块类型: paragraph

**📝 核心内容**
```text
S3000L-B6568-03000-00Applicable to: All S3000L-A-19-00-0000-00A-040A-AChap 19DMC-S3000L-A-19-00-0000-00A-040A-A_000_02_EN-US.doc 2009-06-08 Page 754.13.3 UoF Task - New class and interface definitions4.13.3.1 TaskThe Task class supports the detailed specification of a task, both maintenance tasks as well asoperational tasks.NoteTask is an abstract class, ie an instantiation of Task must be either a Rectifying_task,Operational_task or a Supporting_task. (refer to Chap 12 for definitions and usages of therespective task type).A detailed procedure for performing the task can be documented in zero, one or manyS1000D data modules. The Task UoF supports cross references between tasks beingdefined in S3000L and data modules being produced in accordance with the S1000Dprocedure and schedule schemes, respectively.Task includes its revision identification.Task attributes:Task_identifier (one or many)Task_revision_identifierTask_name (one or many)Task_revision_status (zero or one)Information_codeTask_criticality_code (zero or one)Task_operability_codeTask_duration (zero, one or many)Task_total_labour_time (zero, one or many)S1000D_task_type (zero or one)Task associations:An optional association with zero, one or many succeeding revisions of the taskAn optional association with zero, one or many preceeding revisons of the taskAn optional association with zero, one or many Warning_caution_or_notes (viaTask_warning_caution_or_note)An optional association with zero, one or many S1000D data modules, which contains thedetailed procedures for performing the taskAn optional association with zero, one or many Task_requirements which forms thejustification for the taskAn aggregate association with one or many Subtasks which contains the details for the taskAn optional association with zero, one or many instances of Subtask_by_reference, ieSubtasks that reference the task under consideration as a subtaskNoteThe association between task and S1000D_data_module classes enables the definition ofcross references between the tasks that are defined in S3000L, and the correspondingS1000D data modules.A task in S3000L can be split into many S1000D data modules. One S1000D data modulecan cover many S3000L tasks. However, projects are strongly recommended to aim for aone to one correspondence.Where the concept of master data modules is used in the S1000D CSDB (Common SourceDataBase), this class should just keep the cross references between the S3000L tasks andthe master data modules in the CSDB.Task special recommendations:
```

- -> 下一块: [[#411\|411]]

### 411

**📝 核心内容**
```text
S3000L-B6568-03000-00Applicable to: All S3000L-A-19-00-0000-00A-040A-AChap 19DMC-S3000L-A-19-00-0000-00A-040A-A_000_02_EN-US.doc 2009-06-08 Page 76The value for the Task_duration and Task_total_labour_time attributes can berecommended to use either of the following representations:Value_with_unit_propertyValue_with_limit_property, where Limit_qualifier is set to "maximum".Value_with_tolerances_propertyValue_range_propertyWhere a Task has different Task_durations and/or Task_total_labour_times depending on,eg maintenance level, these should be represented as a set of values of the respectiveattribute, but be distinguished by assigning Applicability_statements to the respective Prprepresenting the individual value (also refer to Para 4.21, UoF Applicability Statement).4.13.3.2 Task_changeThe Task_change class is an association class, ie it adds information to the associationbetween two successive revisions of a rask.Task_change attribute:Task_revision_change_description (zero or one)4.13.3.3 Rectifying_taskThe Rectifying_task class is a specialization of class Task.NoteEach maintenance activity is driven by an event. This event can be a failure, damage,special event or a limit. All these events require a maintenance action to be resolved. Eachtask that is able to resolve such a kind of event should be defined as a rectifying task.Rectifying_task attributes:Task_identifier (inherited from Task)Task_revision_identifier (inherited from Task)Task_name (inherited from Task)Task_revision_status (inherited from Task)Information_code (inherited from Task)Task_criticality_code (inherited from Task)Task_operability_code (inherited from Task)Task_duration (inherited from Task)Task_total_labour_time (inherited from Task)S1000D_task_type (inherited from Task)Rectifying_task associations:An optional association with zero, one or many succeeding revisions of the Rectifying_task(inherited from Task)An optional association with zero, one or many preceeding revisons of the Rectifying_task(inherited from Task)An optional association with zero, one or many Warning_caution_or_notes (viaTask_warning_caution_or_note) (inherited from Task)An optional association with zero, one or many S1000D data modules, which contains thedetailed procedures for performing the Rectifying_task (inherited from Task)An optional association with zero, one or many Task_requirements which forms thejustification for the Rectifying_task (inherited from Task)An aggregate association with one or many Subtasks which contains the details for theRectifying_task (inherited from Task)
```

An example of a supporting task is open hatch, or jack a car.Supporting_task attributes:Task_identifier (inherited from Task)Task_revision_identifier (inherited from Task)Task_name (inherited from Task)Task_revision_status (inherited from Task)Information_code (inherited from Task)Task_criticality_code (inherited from Task)Task_operability_code (inherited from Task)Task_duration (inherited from Task)Task_total_labour_time (inherited from Task)S1000D_task_type (inherited from Task)Supporting_task associations:An optional association with zero, one or many succeeding revisions of the Supporting_task(inherited from Task)An optional association with zero, one or many preceeding revisons of the Supporting_task(inherited from Task)An optional association with zero, one or many Warning_caution_or_notes (viaTask_warning_caution_or_note) (inherited from Task)An optional association with zero, one or many S1000D data modules, which contains thedetailed procedures for performing the Supporting_task (inherited from Task)An optional association with zero, one or many Task_requirements which forms thejustification for the Supporting_task (inherited from Task)An aggregate association with one or many Subtasks which contains the details for theSupporting_task (inherited from Task)An optional association with zero, one or many instances of Subtask_by_reference, ieSubtasks that uses the Supporting_task under consideration as a subtask (inherited fromTask)Supporting_task special recommendations:See special recommendations described under task4.13.3.5 Operational_taskThe Operational_task class is a specialization of class Task.NoteOperational tasks are tasks that are required for operational purposes, eg fueling. Anoperational task may also be used as a subtask within one or many rectifying tasks.Operational_task attributes:

S3000L-B6568-03000-00Applicable to: All S3000L-A-19-00-0000-00A-040A-AChap 19DMC-S3000L-A-19-00-0000-00A-040A-A_000_02_EN-US.doc 2009-06-08 Page 78Task_identifier (inherited from Task)Task_revision_identifier (inherited from Task)Task_name (inherited from Task)Task_revision_status (inherited from Task)Information_code (inherited from Task)Task_criticality_code (inherited from Task)Task_operability_code (inherited from Task)Task_duration (inherited from Task)Task_total_labour_time (inherited from Task)S1000D_task_type (inherited from Task)Operational_task associations:An optional association with zero, one or many succeeding revisions of theOperational_task (inherited from Task)An optional association with zero, one or many preceeding revisons of the Operational_task(inherited from Task)An optional association with zero, one or many Warning_caution_or_notes (viaTask_warning_caution_or_note) (inherited from Task)An optional association with zero, one or many S1000D data modules, which contains thedetailed procedures for performing the Operational_task (inherited from Task)An optional association with zero, one or many Task_requirements which forms thejustification for the Operational_task (inherited from Task)An aggregate association with one or many Subtasks which contains the details for theOperational_task (inherited from Task)An optional association with zero, one or many instances of Subtask_by_reference, ieSubtasks that uses the Operational_task under consideration as a subtask (inherited fromTask)Operational_task special recommendations:See special recommendations described under Task4.13.3.6 SubtaskThe Subtask class defines steps to be performed within a Task. Subtasks are used to providedetailed information about the Task.NoteSubtask is an abstract class, ie an instantiation of Subtask needs to be either aSubtask_by_definition, Subtask_by_reference, or Subtask_by_external_reference.Subtasks within a Task can be time lined using the Subtask_timeline class.Subtask attributes:Subtask_identifierSubtask_role (zero or one)Subtask associations:An association with the Task to which the Subtask belongsAn optional association with zero or one related Subtask, of which the starting point for theSubtask under consideration is dependent (used for time lining of the overall Task egpreceding subtask in a GANTT-schema)An optional association with zero, one or many Subtasks, whose starting points aredependent upon the Subtask under consideration (used for time lining of the overall Task,eg succeeding subtasks in a GANTT-schema)Subtask special recommendations:

### 414

**📊 元数据**
- 所属条号: `段落414`
- 章节路径: `-`
- 字数: 2517 (约1510 tokens)
- 分块类型: paragraph

**📝 核心内容**
```text
S3000L-B6568-03000-00Applicable to: All S3000L-A-19-00-0000-00A-040A-AChap 19DMC-S3000L-A-19-00-0000-00A-040A-A_000_02_EN-US.doc 2009-06-08 Page 79Where a Subtask is dependent upon, eg maintenance level, it shall be distinguished by theassignment of an Applicability_statement to the Subtask (refer to Para 4.21, UoFApplicability Statement)NoteThere might be alternative subtasks within a Task. These are also distinguished by theassignment of Applicability_statements.4.13.3.7 Subtask_timelineThe Subtask_timeline is association class that adds information to the timeline associationbetween two Subtasks. The timeline association enables the definition of time dependenciesbetween two Subtasks in a Task.The Subtask_timeline class defines the event to which relating subtask refer (start or end of therelated Subtask). It also defines a possible lag, ie duration from the time the related eventoccurs and the time the relating Subtask can be initiated (started).Notebe regarded as starting at the beginning of the associated task.This class supports the creation of a GANTT-schema for a task.Subtask_timeline attributes:Subtask_timeline_eventSubtask_timeline_lag4.13.3.8 Subtask_by_referenceThe Subtask_by_reference class is a specialization of subtask. Subtask_by_reference shall beused whereever the Subtask is defined as a task in its own right.NoteSubtask_by_reference is the only mechanism in S3000L that supports reuse of task stepsand/or task procedures in between tasks.Subtask_by_reference attributes:Subtask_identifier (inherited from Subtask)Subtask_role (inherited from Subtask)Subtask_by_reference associations:An association with the Task to which the Subtask_by_reference belongs (inherited fromSubtask)An optional association with zero or one related Subtask, of which the starting point for theSubtask_by_reference under consideration is dependent (inherited from Subtask)An optional association with zero, one or many Subtasks, for which the respective startingpoint is dependent upon the Subtask_by_reference under consideration (inherited fromSubtask)An association with the specific Task being referenced as a SubtaskSubtask_by_reference special recommendations:See special recommendations for Subtask above4.13.3.9 Subtask_by_external_referenceThe Subtask_by_external_reference class is a specialization of Subtask. Subtask_by_external_reference shall be used wherever the complete description of the Subtask is being described inan external source, i.e outside the scope of S3000L and S1000D.
```

ASubtask_by_definition cannot be referenced from any other Task.Subtask_by_definition attributes:Subtask_identifier (inherited from Subtask)Subtask_role (inherited from Subtask)Subtask_revision_identifierSubtask_nameInformation_codeSubtask_description (zero or one)Subtask_duration (one or many)Maintenance_location_code (zero or one)Subtask_by_definition associations:An association with the Task to which the Subtask_by_definition belongs (inherited fromSubtask)An optional association with zero or one related Subtask, of which the starting point for theSubtask_by_definition under consideration is dependent (inherited from Subtask)An optional association with zero, one or many Subtasks, for which the respective startingpoint is dependent upon the Subtask_by_definition under consideration (inherited fromSubtask)An optional association with a zonal location (Zone_element_revision) in which the subtaskis to be performed (via Subtask_in_zone)An optional association with zero, one or many Subtask_acceptance_parameters definingcriteria that need to be fulfilled before the subtask can be closedAn optional association with zero, one or many Breakdown_elements and/or parts whichare the objects on which the subtask is being performedAn optional association with zero, one or many Subtask_objective_states that defines thestates that shall be obtained before the subtask can be closedAn optional association with zero, one or many Warning_caution_or_notes (viaSubtask_warning_caution_or_note)Subtask_by_definition special recommendations:

S3000L-B6568-03000-00Applicable to: All S3000L-A-19-00-0000-00A-040A-AChap 19DMC-S3000L-A-19-00-0000-00A-040A-A_000_02_EN-US.doc 2009-06-08 Page 824.13.3.13 Subtask_target_itemThe Subtask_target_item interface is implemented by the classes that can represent the objecton which a Subtask_by_definition is to be performed.Classes that implement the Subtask_target_item interface:Breakdown_element_revisionPartClasses that implement the Subtask_target_item interface must implement the followingassociation:An optional association with zero, one or many instances of Subtask_by_definition4.13.3.14 Subtask_objective_stateThe Subtask_objective_state class defines a state shall be obtained before the subtask can beclosed.NoteThe use of class Subtask_objective_state has no meaning by itself, but only in the contextof a specific instance of Subtask_by_definition.Subtask_objective_state attributes:Subtask_objective_stateSubtask_ objective_state association:An association with the specific instance of Subtask_by_definition for which theSubtask_objective_state has been definedNoteIf a Subtask_by_definition shall obtain more than one state, there needs to be one instanceof Subtask_objective_state per state.4.13.3.15 Subtask_circuit_breaker_stateThe Subtask_circuit_breaker_state class is a specialization of Subtask_objective_state. TheSubtask_circuit_breaker_state class defines a circuit breaker state that shall be obtained beforethe subtask can be closed. The main difference between the generic Subtask_objective_stateand Subtask_circuit_breaker_state is that the Subtask_circuit_breaker_state identifes theindividual Circuit_breaker that shall obtain the state.NoteThe use of class Subtask_circuit_breaker_state has no meaning by itself, but only in thecontext of a specific instance of Subtask_by_definition.Subtask_circuit_breaker_state attributes:Subtask_objective_state (inherited from Subtask_objective_state)Subtask_circuit_breaker_state associations:An association with the specific instance of Subtask_by_definition for which theSubtask_circuit_breaker_state has been defined (inherited from Subtask_objective_state)An association with the individual Circuit_breaker that shall obtain the stateNoteIf a Subtask_by_definition shall obtain more than one circuit breaker state, there needs tobe one instance of Subtask_circuit_breaker_state per circuit breaker.

S3000L-B6568-03000-00Applicable to: All S3000L-A-19-00-0000-00A-040A-AChap 19DMC-S3000L-A-19-00-0000-00A-040A-A_000_02_EN-US.doc 2009-06-08 Page 834.13.3.16 Circuit_breakerThe Circuit_breaker class identifies individual circuit breakes that are installed on the Product.Circuit_breaker attributes:Circuit_breaker_identifierCircuit_breaker_nameCircuit_breaker_typeCircuit_breaker associations:An optional association wih zero, one or many Subtask_circuit_breaker_states, ie statesthat the circuit breaker shall be in before a specific instance of Subclass_by_definition canbe closedAn association with one or many Products for which the Circuit_breaker is defined (viaCircuit_breaker_in_product)4.13.3.17 Circuit_breaker_in_productThe Circuit_breaker_in_product class defines a relationship between the Circuit_breaker andthe Product on which it is installed.NoteThere is (at least) one instance of Circuit_breaker_in_product per combination ofCircuit_breaker and Product.Circuit_breaker_in_product associations:An association with the identified Circuit_breaker being installed on the ProductAn association with the Product on which the Circuit_breaker is installedAn optional association with a Breakdown_element_revision defining where theCircuit_breaker is localizedCircuit_breaker_in_product special recommendations:If the applicability of the Circuit_breaker or its localization is limitied to a specificProduct_variant, this shall be defined by assigning a Product_variant_applicability to theCircuit_breaker_in_productCircuit_breaker_in_product interfaces:The Circuit_breaker_in_product shall implement the Product_variant_applicability_interfaceNoteIf the Circuit_breaker_in_product is applicable for more than one Product_variant, but notfor all Product_variants, then there must be one assigned Product_variant_applicability perProduct_variant.There can be more than one instance of Circuit_breaker_in_product per combination ofCircuit_breaker and Product, eg where the circuit breaker localization is different fordifferent Product_variants.4.13.3.18 Warning_caution_or_noteThe Warning_caution_or_note class defines advices concerning safety, legal and healthaspects.Warning_caution_or_note attributes:Warning_caution_or_noteWarning_caution_or_note_type (zero or one)Warning_caution_or_note associations:

interface:Subtask_target_item4.13.4.4 Breakdown_element_revisionThe Breakdown_element_revision class defined in UoF Breakdown Structure, must alsoimplement the following additional interface:Subtask_target_itemThe Breakdown_element_revision class defined in UoF Breakdown Structure, must alsoimplement the following additional association:An optional association with zero, one or persubtask, or aggregated per task.Task Resources can be either:Material resources, eg spares, tools, consumablesFacilities, eg hangars, powerPersonnel resources, eg skillsDocumentsMaterial and facility resources can be defined both in terms of specifications, and in terms ofactual parts.

**📝 核心内容**
```text
S3000L-B6568-03000-00Applicable to: All S3000L-A-19-00-0000-00A-040A-AChap 19DMC-S3000L-A-19-00-0000-00A-040A-A_000_02_EN-US.doc 2009-06-08 Page 87The Task_resource_assignment interface enables resources to be assigned to the classes thatimplement this interface.Classes that implement the Task_resource_assignment interface:TaskSubtask_by_definitionThe classes that implement the Task_resource_assignment interface must also implement thefollowing association:An optional association with zero, one or many Task_resources, ie resources needed forperforming the specific instance of Task or Subtask_by_definition4.14.3.2 Task_resourceThe Task_resource class supports the identification of resources needed for the performance ofa specific instance of Task or Subtask_by_definition.NoteTask_resource is an abstract class, ie an instantiation of Task_resource must be either:Task_material_resource_by_specificationTask_materiel_resource_by_referenceTask_facility_resource_by_specificationTask_facility_resource_by_referenceTask_personnel_resourceTask_document_resource.Each instance of Task_resource is uniquely defined for a specific instance of Task orSubtask_by_definition. Reuse of resource definitions is enabled through the use ofResource_specifications, parts, skills, trades and documents.Task_resource associations:An association with a specific instance of Task or Subtask_by_definition, for whichassociated resource is needed (via Task_resource_assignment interface)An optional association where the Task_resource under consideration can relate to otherinstances of Task_resource, eg person A uses toolAn optional association where the Task_resource under consideration can be related tofrom other instances of Task_resource, eg tool B is used by personTask_resource special recommendations:Where a Task_resource is dependent upon eg, maintenance level, external conditions, etc,it shall be distinguished by the assignment of an Applicability_statement (UoF ApplicabilityStatement) to the Task_resource instance4.14.3.3 Task_resource_relationshipThe Task_resource_relationship association class adds information to the Task_resource_relationship association between two instances of Task_resource. The Task_resource_relationship association defines dependencies between two Task_resources in a Task or inSubtask_by_definition, eg person A "uses" tool B.Task_resource_relationship attributes:Task_resource_relationship_category
```

Task_material_resource)Task_material_resource_category (inherited from Task_material_resource)Task_material_resource_by_specification associations:An association with a specific instance of Task or associations:An optional association with zero, one or many parts that realizes theResource_specification, ie actual tools, equipment, spares, that can be used when the taskis to be performed (via an instance of the Resource_realization class)

Resource_specification which has part realizationAn association with a specific part that fulfills the resource specification (realizes thespecification)NoteThere is one instance of Resource_realization per relevant combination ofResource_specification and part.Resource_realization special recommendations:Where a Resource_realization is dependent upon eg, customer, maintenance Material_resource_specificationThe Material_resource_specification class is a specialization of the Resource_specificationclass, and identifies specifications of material resources (spares, consumables, tools, etc.)which can be realized by one or many parts.NoteThe usage of Material_resource_specifications allows for more generic task/subtaskdefinitions, ie the task/subtask does not need (inherited from Resource_specification)Resource_specification_description (inherited from Resource_specification)Material_resource_specification associations:An optional association with zero, one or Task_facility_resourceThe Task_facility_resource class is a specialization of the Task_resource class, and identifiesfacility resources needed for the performance of a specific instance of Task orSubtask_by_definition.NoteTask_facility_resource is an abstract class, ie an instantiation of Task_facility_resourcemust be either:Task_facility_resource_by_specificationTask_facility_resource_by_referenceEach instance of Task_facility_resource is uniquely defined for a specific instance of Taskor Subtask_by_definition. Reuse of facility resource definitions is enabled through the useof Resource_specifications and parts.

or one trade for which the skill has been definedTrade interfaces:The Trade class shall implement the competence interface4.14.3.18 Skill_levelThe Skill_level association class adds information to the association between a skill and a trade.Skill_level attributes:Skill_level_name (zero or one)4.14.3.19 Task_personnel_resource_competenceThe Task_personnel_resource_competence class defines a relationship between an instance ofTask_personnel_resource (ie a needed type of personnel) and a specific Competence (ieinstance of skill or trade) that fulfills that need.Task_personnel_resource_competence associations:An association with an instance of Task_personnel_resource which defines the need of apersonnel resource for a specific instance of Task or Subtask_by_definitionAn association with a specific Competence that fulfills the requirement and can perform thedefined taskAn optional association with zero, one or many Special_training_requirements which areneeded in order for the defined Competence to be able to perform the defined taskNoteThere is one instance of Task_personnel_resource_competence per relevant combinationof Task_personnel_resource and Competence (ie trade or skill).Task_personnel_resource_competence special recommendations:Where a specified Task_personnel_resource_competence is dependent upon eg customer,maintenance level, or external conditions, it shall be distinguished by the assignment of anApplicability_statement to the Task_personnel_resource_competence instance (refer toPara 4.21, UoF Applicability statement)4.14.3.20 Special_training_requirementThe Special_training_requirement class identifes additional training required for the givenCompetence, in order for the defined Competence to be able to perform the defined task.NoteThe use of class Special_training_requirement has no meaning by itself, but only in thecontext of an association between a specific required personnel resource, and a definedcompetence (ie in association with an instance of Task_personnel_resource_ competence).Special_training_requirement attributes:Special_training_requirement_descriptionSpecial_training_category (zero or one)Special_training_requirement associations:An instance of Special_training_requirement is always associated with a specific instanceof Task_personnel_resource_competence (ie the association between a needed personnelresource, and a defined competence), for which the Special_training_requirement has beendefined

### 433

**📊 元数据**
- 所属条号: `段落433`
- 章节路径: `-`
- 字数: 2322 (约1393 tokens)
- 分块类型: paragraph

**📝 核心内容**
```text
S3000L-B6568-03000-00Applicable to: All S3000L-A-19-00-0000-00A-040A-AChap 19DMC-S3000L-A-19-00-0000-00A-040A-A_000_02_EN-US.doc 2009-06-08 Page 98Classes that implement the Task_target_item interface:Breakdown_element_revisionPartClasses that implement the Task_target_item interface must implement the followingassociations:An optional association with zero, one or many instances of Task_usage (and subclassesthereof)NoteSince the Task_target_item interface is implemented by Breakdown_element_revision andPart, it means that instances of the following classes can be selected asTask_target_items; Hardware_element_revision, Software_element_revision,Aggregated_element_revision, Zone_element_revision, Hardware_part and Software_partrespectively.4.15.3.2 Task_usageThe Task_usage class (specifically its subclasses) identifies the Task that is to be performed ona Breakdown_element_revision or Part (ie the Task_target_item).NoteTask_usage is an abstract class, ie an instantiation of Task_usage needs to be either aTask_plan or Supporting_task_usage (refer to Para 4.16, UoF Task Usage (Part 2)).Task_usage associations:An association with the Task_target_item (instance of Breakdown_element_revision orPart) on which the associated task is to be performedAn optional association with zero, one or many instances of Task_frequency4.15.3.3 Task_frequencyThe Task_frequency class defines the frequency of performance or occurrence for theassociated task.NoteThe use of class Task_frequency has no meaning by itself, but only in the context of aTask_usage.Task_frequency attributes:Task_frequencyTask_frequency_calculation_method (zero or one)Task_frequency associations:An instance of Task_frequency is always associated with a specific instance ofTask_usage, ie a specific combination of task and the object on which the task is to beperformed.Task_frequency special recommendations:Where a Task_frequency is dependent upon eg external conditions, customer, etc, it shallbe distinguished by the assignment of an Applicability_statement to the Task_frequencyinstance (also refer to Para 4.21, UoF Applicability Statement).4.15.3.4 Planned_task_itemThe Planned_task_item interface is implemented by the classes that can be part of aTask_plan, ie be associated with task limits and preferred locations for task execution.
```

S3000L-B6568-03000-00Applicable to: All S3000L-A-19-00-0000-00A-040A-AChap 19DMC-S3000L-A-19-00-0000-00A-040A-A_000_02_EN-US.doc 2009-06-08 Page 99Classes that implement the Planned_task_item interface:Rectifying_taskOperational_taskClasses that implement the Planned_task_item interface must also implement the followingassociations:An association with one or many instances of Task_plan4.15.3.5 Task_planThe Task_plan class is a specialization of the Task_usage class, and adds information in termsof task limits and preferred locations for task execution.NoteThere is one instance of Task_plan per relevant combination of Planned_task_item andBreakdown_element_revision or Part.Task_plan associations:An association with the item (instance of Breakdown_element_revision or Part via theTask_target_item interface) on which the associated Planned_task_item is to be performed(inherited from Task_usage).An optional association with zero, one or many instances of Task_frequency (inherited fromTask_usage).An association with the Planned_task_item (instance of Rectifying_task orOperational_task) which is to be performed on the Task_target_item (instance ofBreakdown_element_ revision or Part).An association with at least one instance of Maintenance_level_allocation, ie locationwhere the associated Planned_task_item is to be performed.An optional association with zero, one or many instances of Task_limit, ie thresholds and/orintervals defining when the associated Planned_task_item is to be performed.Task_plan recommendations:Where a Task_plan is dependent upon eg external conditions, customer, etc, it shall bedistinguished by the assignment of an Applicability_statement to the Task_plan instance(also refer to Para 4.21, UoF Applicability Statement).4.15.3.6 Maintenance_level_allocationThe Maintenance_level_allocation class defines a relationship between an instance ofTask_plan and an identified support organization, where the associated Planned_task_item is tobe carried out (via the Maintenance_level_allocation_ interface).NoteThe use of class Maintenance_level_allocation has no meaning by itself, but only in thecontext of a Task_plan.Maintenance_level_allocation associations:An instance of Maintenance_level_allocation is always associated with an instance ofTask_plan, ie specific combination of Planned_task_item and Breakdown_element_revision or Part.An association with the Maintenance_level_type, Maintenance_location,Operating_location_type, or Operating_location that is identified as the organization thatshall have the capability to perform the associated task (via the Maintenance_level_allocation_interface).

### 437

- 章节路径: `-`

- 分块类型: paragraph

**📝 核心内容**
```text
S3000L-B6568-03000-00Applicable to: All S3000L-A-19-00-0000-00A-040A-AChap 19DMC-S3000L-A-19-00-0000-00A-040A-A_000_02_EN-US.doc 2009-06-08 Page 102Discrete_task_limit must not contain a computable expression, ie it can only contain aTask_limit_description without any computable triggers or thresholds.Discrete_task_limit attributes:Task_limit_harmonization_indicator (inherited from Task_limit)Task_limit_description (inherited from Task_limit)Discrete_task_limit associations:An instance of Discrete_task_limit is always associated with a specific instance ofTask_plan, ie a specific combination of Planned_task_item and the object on which thePlanned_task_item is to be performed (inherited from Task_limit)An optional association with an instance of Sampling (inherited from Task_limit)An optional trigger association with zero, one or many instances of Threshold_definitionAn optional threshold association with zero, one or many instances of Threshold_definitionNoteThe threshold association defines a specified interval, from a defined event, to the nextrequired performance of the Planned_task_item (ie Rectifying_task or Operational_task).The defined event is defined by the trigger association.The trigger association describes the event to which the threshold is related, e.g bird strikeor engine replacement. If the Discrete_task_limit does not define any trigger association, itshall be assumed that the trigger event equals the Hardware_maintenence_start defined forHardware_part in UoF Part. The trigger activates the threshold.Multiple instances of Treshold_definitions for both the trigger and the threshold associationDiscrete_task_limit special recommendations:Where a Discrete_task_limit is dependent upon eg external conditions, customer, etc, itshall be distinguished by the assignment of an Applicability_statement to theDiscrete_task_limit instance (also also refer to Para 4.21, UoF Applicability Statement)NoteThe Discrete_task_limit can be used to define "Perform once" and "On condition" task limitsas defined in S1000D.4.15.3.13 Periodic_task_limitThe Periodic_task_limit class is a specialization of the Task_limit class, and is used to identifytask limits that are repeated with a specific interval, ie there is a next scheduled occurrence forthe defined task limit.NoteThe use of class Periodic_task_limit has no meaning by itself, but only in the context of aspecific instance of Task_plan plan (ie specific combination of Planned_task_item andTask_target_item).Periodic_task_limit shall contain a computable expression.Periodic_task_limit attributes:Task_limit_harmonization_indicator (inherited from Task_limit)Task_limit_description (inherited from Task_limit)Periodic_task_limit associations:
```

```text
S3000L-B6568-03000-00Applicable to: All S3000L-A-19-00-0000-00A-040A-AChap 19DMC-S3000L-A-19-00-0000-00A-040A-A_000_02_EN-US.doc 2009-06-08 Page 103An instance of Periodic_task_limit is always associated with a specific instance ofTask_plan, ie a specific combination of Planned_task_item and the object on which thePlanned_task_item is to be performed (inherited from Task_limit)An optional association with an instance of Sampling (inherited from Task_limit)An optional initial threshold association with zero, one or many instances ofThreshold_definitionAn association with one or many instances of Repeat_task_limitsNoteThe initial_threshold association defines a specified interval for the first requiredperformance of the Planned_task_item (ie Rectifying_task or Operational_task). Theinterval shall be assumed to be triggered by the condition that is defined as theHardware_maintenence_start for Hardware_part in UoF Part.Multiple instances of Threshold_definitions for the initial_threshold association meansMultiple instances of Repeat_task_limits for the repeat association requires that eachinstance of Repeat_task_limits is ordered using the Subsequent_repeat_relationship class.Periodic_task_limit special recommendations:Where a Periodic_task_limit is dependent upon eg external conditions, customer, etc, itshall be distinguished by the assignment of an Applicability_statement to thePeriodic_task_limit instance (also refer to Para 4.21, UoF Applicability Statement)NoteThe Periodic_task_limit class can be used to define explicit combinations of "Perform once"
and "Perform periodically" task limits as defined in S1000D.4.15.3.14 Repeat_task_limitThe Repeat_task_limit class defines task limits that are repeated with a specific interval.NoteThe use of class Repeat_task_limit has no meaning by itself, but only in the context of aspecific Periodic_task_limit.Repeat_task_limit associations:An instance of Repeat_task_limit is always associated with a specific instance ofPeriodic_task_limitA threshold association with one or many instances of Threshold_definitionAn optional association with zero or one succeeding instance of Repeat_task_limit, via theSubsequent_repeat_relationshipAn optional association with zero or one preceeding instance of Repeat_task_limit, via theSubsequent_repeat_relationshipNoteThe threshold association defines a specified interval for the repeated performance of thePlanned_task_item (ie Rectifying_task or Operational_task).verThe Repeat_task_limit class can be used to define "Perform periodically" task limits asdefined in S1000D Issue 3.0.
```

Threshold_definition needsto be either a Parameter_threshold or an Event_threshold.Threshold_definition associations:An instance of Threshold_definition can only be associated with one specific constitutes a threshold or a trigger.NoteThe use of class Parameter_threshold has no meaning by itself, but only in the context of aspecific instace of either:Discrete_task_limit, as a trigger

```text
S3000L-B6568-03000-00Applicable to: All S3000L-A-19-00-0000-00A-040A-AChap 19DMC-S3000L-A-19-00-0000-00A-040A-A_000_02_EN-US.doc 2009-06-08 Page 105Discrete_task_limit, as a thresholdPeriodic_task_limit, as an initial thresholdRepeat_task_limit, as a thresholdSubsequent_repeat_relationship, as a trigger.An example of Parameter_threshold is 1000 flight hours. This value can either be a triggeror a threshold depending on how it is being referenced.Parameter_threshold attributes:Threshold_valueParameter_threshold associations:An instance of Parameter_threshold is always associated with a specific instance of one ofof the following:Discrete_task_limit, as a triggerDiscrete_task_limit, as a thresholdPeriodic_task_limit, as an initial thresholdRepeat_task_limit, as a thresholdSubsequent_repeat_relationship, as a triggerParameter_threshold special recommendations:Representations of the Threshold_value attribute is recommended to use thebe cases where the use of Value_with_tolerances or Value_range_property is valid4.15.3.18 Event_thresholdThe Event_threshold class is a specialization of class Threshold_definition and is used torepresent the number of occurrences of an event that constitutes the threshold.NoteThe use of class Event_threshold has no meaning by itself, but only in the context of aspecific instace of either:Discrete_task_limit, as a triggerDiscrete_task_limit, as a thresholdPeriodic_task_limit, as an initial thresholdRepeat_task_limit, as a threshold.Subsequent_repeat_relationship, as a triggerEvent_threshold is an abstract class, ie an instantiation of Event_threshold needs to beeither a Failure_or_special_event_related_threshold or a Task_related_threshold.Event_threshold attributes:Event_threshold_number_of_event_occurrencesEvent_threshold associations:An instance of Event_threshold is always associated with a specific instance of one of theof the following:Discrete_task_limit, as a triggerDiscrete_task_limit, as a thresholdPeriodic_task_limit, as an initial thresholdRepeat_task_limit, as a thresholdSubsequent_repeat_relationship, as a trigger
```

This UoF adds the ability to define any type of additionalorganizational information which is of relevance for an instance of the classes thatimplement the Organization_assignment_interface. An example of additional organizationala specific part. This is not explicitin the data model but can be added using this UoF, by defining theOrganization_assignment_role of class Organization_assignment as4.18.2 Graphical representationICN-B6865-S3000L0232-001-01Fig 33 UoF Organization Assignment - class model4.18.3 UoF Organization Assignment - New class and interface definitions4.18.3.1 Organization_assignment_interfaceThe Organization_assignment_interface is implemented by the classes that can be associatedwith optional organizational information.

S3000L-B6568-03000-00Applicable to: All S3000L-A-19-00-0000-00A-040A-AChap 19DMC-S3000L-A-19-00-0000-00A-040A-A_000_02_EN-US.doc 2009-06-08 Page 1164.19 UoF Document 4.19.1 Overall descriptionThe Document UoF supports a flexible way of assigning documents to different types of objectsin the data model.NoteThere are some previously proposed (even mandatory) associations with the Documentclass in the data model. This UoF adds the ability to define any type of additional documentinformation which is of relevance for an instance of the classes that implement theDocument_assignment_interface. An example of additional document information can bepart. This is not explicit in the data model but can be added usingthis UoF, by defining the Document_assignment_role of class Document_assignment asThe S3000L data model defines two specializations of document:S1000D data modules, which just contain information for being able to identify thedocumentExternal documents, which contains more meta information about the document itself

S3000L-B6568-03000-00Applicable to: All S3000L-A-19-00-0000-00A-040A-AChap 19DMC-S3000L-A-19-00-0000-00A-040A-A_000_02_EN-US.doc 2009-06-08 Page 1174.19.2 Graphical representationICN-B6865-S3000L0233-001-01Fig 34 UoF Document - class model4.19.3 UoF Document - New class and interface definitions4.19.3.1 DocumentThe Document class represents documents which are of relevance for the LSA program.

theDocument_assignment_interface (via Document_assignment and Document_assignment_interface)4.19.3.2 S1000D_data_moduleThe S1000D_data_module class is a specialization of class Document.The S1000D_data_module class enables the cross references in between the Tasks beingdefined in S3000L, and its coresponding S1000D data modules, as defined in UoF Task.However, a document that is of type S1000D_data_module can also be assigned to zero, oneor many instances of classes that implement the Document_assignment_interface, for the samereason as any other type of document.S1000D_data_module attributes:Data_module_codeData_module_issue_number (zero or one)S1000D_data_module association:An optional association with zero, one or many instances of any class that implements theDocument_assignment_interface (inherited from class Document)4.19.3.3 External_documentThe External_document class is a specialization of class Document, and represents alldocuments that are not identified and produced in accordance with S1000D.External_document attributes:Document_identifier (one or many)Document_titleDocument_issue_identifier (zero or one)Document_issue_date (zero or one)Document_type (zero or one)Document_location (zero or one)External_document associations:An optional association with zero, one or many instances of any class that implements theDocument_assignment_interface (inherited from class Document)4.19.3.4 Document_assignment_interfaceThe Document_assignment_interface is implemented by the classes that can be associatedwith optional document information.Classes that implement the Document_assignment_interface:ProjectContracted_product_variantContractProductProduct_variantOrganizationOperating_location

S3000L-B6568-03000-00Applicable to: All S3000L-A-19-00-0000-00A-040A-AChap 19DMC-S3000L-A-19-00-0000-00A-040A-A_000_02_EN-US.doc 2009-06-08 Page 120Classes that implement the Document_assignment_interface must implement the followingassociation:An optional association with zero, one or many instances of documents (via theDocument_assignment class)4.19.3.5 Document_assignmentThe Document_assignment class defines a relationship between a specific document and aninstance of any class that implements the Document_assignment_interface.NoteThe role of the document being assigned is determined by Document_assignment_roleattribute.The Document_portion attribute identifies a defined portion of a document which is ofinterest in a specific usage.Document_assignment attributes:Document_assignment_roleDocument_portion (zero or one)Document associations:An association with the specific document being assignedAn association with the object to which the instance of document is being assigned (aninstance of any class that implements the Document_assignment_interface)NoteThere is one instance of Document_assignment per relevant combination of Document andinstance to which the Document is assigned (ie relevant instance of any class thatimplements the Document_assignment_interface).4.19.4 UoF Document - Additions to referenced class and interface definitions4.19.4.1 ProjectThe Project class defined in UoF Project, must also implement the following additional interface:Document_assignment_interface4.19.4.2 Contracted_product_variantThe Contracted_product_variant class defined in UoF Project, must also implement thefollowing additional interface:Document_assignment_interface4.19.4.3 ContractThe Contract class defined in UoF Project, must also implement the following additionalinterface:Document_assignment_interface4.19.4.4 ProductThe Product class defined in UoF Project, must also implement the following additionalinterface:Document_assignment_interface4.19.4.5 Product_variantThe Product_variant class defined in UoF Project, must also implement the following additionalinterface:

### 470

**📊 元数据**
- 所属条号: `段落470`
- 章节路径: `-`
- 字数: 2785 (约1671 tokens)
- 分块类型: paragraph

**📝 核心内容**
```text
S3000L-B6568-03000-00Applicable to: All S3000L-A-19-00-0000-00A-040A-AChap 19DMC-S3000L-A-19-00-0000-00A-040A-A_000_02_EN-US.doc 2009-06-08 Page 1374.20.4.74 Serial_number_rangeThe Serial_number_range class defined in UoF Applicability Statement, must also implementthe following additional interface:Remark_assignment_interface4.21 UoF Applicability Statement 4.21.1 Overall descriptionThe Applicability Statement UoF defines a flexible way of assigning applicability statements toinstances of relevant classes in the data model. This UoF supports simple to highly complexdefinitions of applicability.NoteThe S3000L applicability statement class model is influenced by the applicability modeldefined in S1000D.The Applicability Statement class model supports:Identification of parameters that can affect the applicability of LSA data (eg parameterssuch as "model A", "tail number 15", "arctic", "desert", "land-based", "sea-based", etc)Creation of computable logical expressions which can be evaluated, using the identifiedparametersEnumeration of the S3000L classes that can have an associated applicability statementThe definition of an applicability statement is based upon the following constructs:Applicability assignment, which identifies the classes to which an applicability statementcan be assignedApplicability evaluation, which defines the expression to be evaluated in order for the targetitem to be applicableThe applicability expression to be evaluated can be either a logical expression, or a value to beasserted. Any use of a logical expression will in turn relate to one or many values which are tobe asserted.NoteS1000D defines two separate types of parameters which can affect the applicability of techdata. Firstly, product attributes and secondly, conditions. Product attributes are propertiesof the product that are typically set at the time of manufacture of a product instance and willnot change throughout the service life of a product instance. Examples of product attributesenvironmental or any other type that can change throughout the service life of a productinstance. Examples of conditions given in S1000D are location of maintenance,temperature, wind speed and sandy conditions. However, there is no hard and fast rule inS1000D for distinguishing between product attributes and conditions, which is why S3000Ldoes not implement this distinction in the data model. Mapping in between S3000Lparameters and S1000D product attributes and conditions needs to be done on a perproject basis.Applicability_statement is used to define usage related restrictions, whereasProduct_variant_applicability and Product_variant_realization_applicability (UoF ProductVariant Applicability) are used to define restrictions for a given product design (productdefinition).
```

### 472

**📊 元数据**

- 字数: 2295 (约1377 tokens)

**📝 核心内容**
```text
S3000L-B6568-03000-00Applicable to: All S3000L-A-19-00-0000-00A-040A-AChap 19DMC-S3000L-A-19-00-0000-00A-040A-A_000_02_EN-US.doc 2009-06-08 Page 1394.21.3 UoF Applicability Statement - New class and interface definitions4.21.3.1 Condition_typeThe Condition_type class defines types of conditions to be used in applicability statements,where the specific type of condition to be used can not be derived from any other class beingdefined within the S3000L data model.NoteExamples of condition types that can not be derived from the use of any other class in theS3000L data model, and therefore needs to be defined as instances of Condition_type, are:Service bulletinOperational_environmentOperational_scenarioOperational_stateAn example of a class in the data model which can be used to define a condition is thecustomer class. The values that can be used in a condition are the instances of that class,ie the values already exists in the LSA database.Condition_type attributes:Condition_typeCondition_type associations:An association with one or many Condition_type_valuesAn optional association with zero, one or many instances of ConditionNoteThe following rules can be applied to populate an S1000D Condition Cross-referemceTable (CCT):The value of Condition_type can populate the <conditiontype id> attribute and<conditiontype><name> element, respectivelyThe definition of the class in an RDL can be used to populate the<conditiontype><description> element4.21.3.2 Condition_type_valueThe Condition_type_value class defines valid values for a specific Condition_type.NoteInstances of the Condition_type_value has no meaning by itself, but only in the context of aspecific Condition_type, or a specific Condition_statement.Examples on condition type values are:Pre/Post (can be defined as valid values for eg the service bulletin condition type)True/False (can be defined as valid values for eg the operational_environment,operational_scenario or operational_state condition types)Condition_type_value attributes:Condition_type_valueCondition_type_value associations:An instance of Condition_type_value is always associated with either a specificCondition_type, or a specific Condition_statementNoteThe following rules can be applied to populate an S1000D Condition Cross-reference Table(CCT):
```

```text
S3000L-B6568-03000-00Applicable to: All S3000L-A-19-00-0000-00A-040A-AChap 19DMC-S3000L-A-19-00-0000-00A-040A-A_000_02_EN-US.doc 2009-06-08 Page 140The value of Condition_type_value can populate the <conditiontype><enum> elementfor the associated Condition_type4.21.3.3 ConditionThe Condition class identifies specific conditions which have an effect on the applicability of thedata being defined during LSA.NoteThe Condition class is an abstract class, ie any of its subclasses must be used for theactual representation of the specified condition.Condition subclasses:Condition_by_classCondition_by_identificationCondition_type_value associations:An instance of Condition is always associated with a specific Condition_typeAn instance of Condition can be used in zero, one or many Condition_statements4.21.3.4 Condition_by_classCondition_by_class identifies specific classes that can be used as conditions in one or manyapplicability statements.Condition_by_class is a specialization of class Condition.Note:Examples on condition class values are:Arctic/Desert (can be defined as valid conditions for the operational_environmentcondition type)War_time/Peace_time (can be defined as valid conditions for the operational_scenariocondition type)In_operation/In_storage (can be defined as valid values for eg the operational_statecondition type)Condition_by_class attributes:Condition_classCondition_by_class associations:An instance of Condition_by_class is always associated with a specific Condition_type(inherited from class Condition)An instance of Condition_by_class can be used in zero, one or many Condition_statements(inherited from class Condition)NoteThe following rules can be applied to populate an S1000D Condition Cross-referemceTable (CCT):The value of Condition_class can populate the <condition id> attributeThe description of the class in an RDL can populate the <condition><description>elementThe value of Condition_class can also populate the <condition><name>and<condition><displayname> elements respectively

S3000L-B6568-03000-00Applicable to: All S3000L-A-19-00-0000-00A-040A-AChap 19DMC-S3000L-A-19-00-0000-00A-040A-A_000_02_EN-US.doc 2009-06-08 Page 1414.21.3.5 Condition_by_identifierThe Condition_by_identifier class identifies specific instances of a condition type that can beused as conditions in one or many applicability statements.Condition_by_identifier is a specialization of class Condition.Note:An example of an identified condition is:Service bulletin S001 - Chain guard (an identified instance of the service bulletincondition type)Condition_by_identifier attributes:Condition_identifierCondition_descriptionCondition_nameCondition_by_identifier associations:An instance of Condition_by_identifier is always associated with a specific Condition_type(inherited from class Condition)An instance of Condition_by_identifier can be used in zero, one or manyCondition_statements (inherited from class Condition)NoteThe following rules can be applied to populate an S1000D Condition Cross-referemceTable (CCT):The value of Condition_ identifier can populate the <condition id> attributeThe value of Condition_description can populate the <condition><description>The value of Condition_name can populate the <condition><name>and<condition><displayname> respectively4.21.3.6 Applicability_statementThe Applicability_statement class defines explicit applicability statements to be used againstinstances in the LSA database.NoteAn Applicability_statement can be evaluated in a maintenance execution environment, egusing an Interactive Electronic Technical Publishing (IETP) application using S1000D datamodules.Applicability_statement attributes:Applicability_statement_identifier (zero or one)Applicability_statement_descriptionApplicability_statement associations:An optional association with zero or one computer interpretable expression, ie an instanceof Applicability_evaluationAn association with one or many objects for which the Applicability_statement is valid, i.einstances of any class that implements the Applicability_assignment interfaceNoteThe Applicability_statement class can be specialized into a Dated_applicability_statement.

evaluated, but is also limited in time.Dated_applicability_statement attributes:Applicability_statement_identifier (inherited from Applicability_statement)Applicability_statement_description ieinstances of any class that implements the Applicability_assignment interface (inheritedfrom Applicability_statement)4.21.3.8 Applicability_assignmentThe Applicability_assignment interface is implemented by the classes that can be associatedwith optional applicability statements.Classes that shall implement the Applicability_assignment interface:Hardware_part_operational_authorized_lifeParts_list_entryKey_performance_parameterMean_time_between_failure_correctionLSA_candidate_technology_behavior_ratingLSA_candidate_failure_modeFailure_mode_failure_cause_associationFailure_mode_effectFailure_mode_detection_and_localizationFailure_or_special_eventTestabilityTask_requirementTaskSubtaskSubtask_timelineSubtask_in_zoneTask_warning_caution_or_noteSubtask_warning_caution_or_noteSubtask_acceptance_parameterSubtask_objective_stateTask_resourceTask_resource_relationshipResource_realizationTask_personnel_resource_competenceSpecial_training_requirementTask_usageTask_frequencyMaintenance_level_allocationTask_limitSecurity_classification
